value.text:
The 50/30/20 rule is simple:

50% needs
30% wants
20% savings

But most people never track whether they actually follow it.

Start by logging every transaction for just one week. You'll be surprised where your money really goes.

Subscriptions are the silent budget killer.

The average person spends $219/month on subscriptions they forgot about.

Quick audit:
- Open your bank statement
- Highlight every recurring charge
- Ask: "Would I sign up for this today?"

If not, cancel it. Today.

Two debt payoff strategies:

Snowball: Pay smallest debt first. Wins feel good. Builds momentum.

Avalanche: Pay highest interest first. Saves more money long-term.

Both work. Pick the one you'll actually stick with.

Consistency beats optimization every time.

Your net worth is not your salary.

Net worth = what you own - what you owe

Someone earning $40k with $20k saved has a higher net worth than someone earning $120k with $50k in debt.

Track it monthly. Even if the number is negative. Awareness is step one.

5 money habits that changed my finances:

1. Track every expense (yes, even coffee)
2. Automate savings on payday
3. Review subscriptions monthly
4. Set 3 specific savings goals
5. Check net worth on the 1st of each month

Small systems beat big willpower.

tweets:
I track every dollar I earn and spend inside Notion.

Here's the exact system I use (and why I stopped paying for finance apps):

[thread]

1/ Income & Expense Tracker

Every transaction gets logged with category, payment method, and date.

I can filter by month, by category, or by type (income vs expense).

Takes 30 seconds per entry. I do it right after every purchase.

2/ Subscription Manager

I list every recurring charge with its renewal date.

A formula automatically warns me when something renews within 7 days.

Last month I caught a $22 app I hadn't used in 3 months. Cancelled immediately.

3/ Savings Goals with Progress Bars

Each goal shows a visual progress bar.

Seeing "Emergency Fund: 62% ████████░░" is way more motivating than a number in a spreadsheet.

I contribute to goals on payday. Non-negotiable.

4/ Debt Payoff + Net Worth

I track every debt with its interest rate and payment schedule.

At the end of each month, I log a snapshot: total assets, total liabilities, net worth.

Watching that number trend up is addictive (in a good way).

I packaged this entire system into a Notion template: https://tatsuya27.gumroad.com/l/personal-finance-dashboard
